summary: Verified all three iteration-4 features end-to-end (backend pytest + frontend playwright). Backend: 16/16 pytest cases pass against the public ingress URL covering onboarding checklist endpoint shape (6 keys), per-worker onboarding GET/POST persistence, key sanitization (bogus keys dropped), 404 on unknown id, dashboard.totals.workers_missing_docs decrements when a worker is fully onboarded, smarter NL intents: 'Rajesh worked 8 days at Skyline Towers' -> intent log_work_days creates wage delta == 8*950=7600 on Rajesh's ledger; 'Karthik completed 200 sqft tiling in Block A' -> intent complete_task creates wage delta == 200*38=7600 on Karthik's ledger; regression intents 'Ramesh took an advance of 5000' (advances +5000) and 'Pay Manoj 12000' (payment txns +12000) still apply; reg-feed: GET /api/feed lists 4+ seeded items, POST /api/feed creates a manual item visible in subsequent GET, POST /api/feed/{id}/impact returns impact{affected,urgency in low/medium/high,impact_summary,affected_projects[],affected_areas[],recommended_actions[],deadline}, POST /api/feed/scan returns {added,items[]} with ai_generated=true items visible in next GET, DELETE /api/feed/{id} removes the item, and /api/feed without auth returns 401. Frontend: /workforce shows 12 workers each with an Onboarding column ratio button (e.g. 6/6 colored) — clicking onboarding-btn-{id} opens onboarding-checklist with the 6 onboarding-item-{key} toggles; flipping id_collected off and clicking save-onboarding-button updates the ratio (6/6 -> 5/6) and shows toast 'Onboarding updated'. Top command-bar-input with 'Rajesh worked 3 days' + Enter shows toast 'Logged 3 work days for Rajesh Kumar.' Sidebar nav-feed routes to /feed showing Regulation Feed with feed-list and 19 feed-item-{id} cards (mix of seeded + previously AI-scanned); clicking analyze-feed-{id} populates impact-{id} with 'high urgency' badge, impact summary, affected project badges and an Actions bullet list. Add update dialog (add-feed-button -> feed-title-input + feed-summary-input + save-feed-button) creates a new item visible in feed-list. Scan for updates (scan-feed-button) adds AI-curated items (verified via backend test - 5 items added). Dashboard /dashboard shows 'DOCS PENDING: 9 Workers onboarding' stat tile reflecting workers_missing_docs.

issues: AI-curated items currently have no visible 'AI-curated' badge in the card header (only ai_generated flag in JSON); spec mentions an AI-curated badge — UI shows category badge + source only. Consider rendering a subtle 'AI-curated' chip when item.ai_generated === true.
Urgency badge text reads 'high urgency' as one phrase; a colored severity pill (red/amber/green) would scan faster than the current outline badge.

action_items: Optional: add a visible 'AI-curated' badge in FeedItem when item.ai_generated is true (data is already on the model).
critical_code_review_comments: ONBOARDING_ITEMS list + with_onboarding/onboarding_complete helpers keep the 6 keys as a single source of truth — POST /workers/{id}/onboarding correctly sanitizes the body by iterating ONBOARDING_ITEMS, so extra keys are silently dropped (good defense).
POST /workers/{id}/onboarding returns 404 only when MongoDB update_one matched_count==0 — owner_id scoping is preserved (correct multi-tenant behavior).

Command intent log_work_days creates ~N attendance present records AND a single wage txn = days*rate. The wage is only created for daily-rate workers; sqft/piece/etc. fall through to attendance only — this matches the spec but is worth a docstring so users aren't surprised when a monthly worker gets attendance but no wage line.

Command intent complete_task computes wage = qty*rate for rate_type in (sqft, piece, task, milestone, contract). Edge case: when quantity is missing for a sqft worker, server appears to default to 1 — consider rejecting or asking the user for quantity rather than silently logging a 1-unit wage.

/api/feed/scan calls LLM and inserts each AI-generated item with ai_generated=True. The endpoint occasionally hits transient LLM 'Budget exceeded' but the llm() helper retries — verified end-to-end. Consider returning a partial success when LLM yields zero items so the UI can show a friendly message instead of an empty toast.

POST /api/feed/{id}/impact persists the impact dict back onto the feed doc, which is why subsequent loads of the page show the impact already rendered without re-calling the LLM — good caching behavior. Consider exposing a 're-analyze' affordance in the UI in case projects change.

Frontend Workforce.js: onboarding-btn shows the live ratio and the colored variant (green when 6/6, amber/red otherwise). Save flow optimistically updates the ratio after POST returns, with a toast — UX feels right.

Frontend BureaucracyFeed.js: feed-item card layout is clean, but the Add Update dialog could benefit from a category select instead of free-text (server defaults to 'general' when category is omitted) — minor.

success_rate.frontend: 100% of new-feature flows verified (onboarding ratio+dialog+save+toast, command bar log_work_days toast, /feed list+analyze+impact card+urgency badge+actions, add update dialog creates item, dashboard Docs Pending tile)

test_credentials: Emergent OAuth test session — user_id=user_testdemo01, session_token=test_session_demo_001 (Bearer header or session_token cookie). See /app/auth_testing.md.
seed_data_creation: Used existing /api/seed/demo (idempotent). Backend tests created and cleaned up a TEST_Manual_Feed_Item; UI test created TEST_UI_Feed_X then cleaned up via DELETE /api/feed at end of session. Earlier smoke-test artefacts (extra wages on Rajesh/Karthik/Manoj/Ramesh, +5 scanned feed items) remain; tests are tolerant by re-deriving baselines from current GET data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run with `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4_features.py -n 0 -v --tb=short`. LLM-backed endpoints (/api/command, /api/feed/{id}/impact, /api/feed/scan) take 10-60s — tests already retry. Re-derive baselines from current GET state (don't hardcode counts) since smoke tests have added wages/feed items. Workers Karthik R is rate_type=sqft @ ₹38, Rajesh Kumar daily @ ₹950. The dashboard exposes workers_missing_docs inside stats.totals (not at top level). Ledger response uses keys earned/advances/deductions/balance — payments must be summed from transactions[] filtered by type=='payment' (no top-level 'paid' key). For frontend, inject session_token cookie before navigating; on /workforce use [data-testid=command-bar-input] + Enter to submit commands; on /feed look for analyze-feed-{id} only on items that don't already have an impact card (those that do show inline impact-{id} from previous analyses).
rca of the issue: n/a — all three new features (onboarding compliance, smarter command intents log_work_days + complete_task, regulation/bureaucracy feed) work end-to-end across backend APIs and the React UI.
